questions.question: Find the determinant of the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 4 & -2 \\ 3 & 5 \end{pmatrix}$.
questions.explanation: For a 2×2 matrix $\begin{pmatrix} a & b \\ c & d \end{pmatrix}$, the determinant is $ad - bc$. Here: $4(5) - (-2)(3) = 20 + 6 = 26$.

questions.question: Multiply the matrices $\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 3 \end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} 1 & 4 \\ -1 & 2 \end{pmatrix}$. What is the entry in row 1, column 2?
questions.explanation: Row 1, column 2 comes from row 1 of the first matrix times column 2 of the second: $2(4) + 1(2) = 8 + 1 = 9$.

questions.question: A matrix $A = \begin{pmatrix} 3 & 2 \\ 1 & 4 \end{pmatrix}$ is multiplied by a scalar 2. What is the determinant of $2A$?
questions.explanation: When a 2×2 matrix is multiplied by scalar $k$, the determinant is multiplied by $k^2$. Original det: $3(4) - 2(1) = 10$. So $\det(2A) = 2^2 \cdot 10 = 40$.

questions.question: If $\begin{pmatrix} x & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{pmatrix} \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ -1 \end{pmatrix} = \begin{pmatrix} 0 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$, find the value of $x$.
questions.explanation: Multiply row 1: $x(1) + 2(-1) = 0$, so $x - 2 = 0$, giving $x = 2$. Check with row 2: $3(1) + 1(-1) = 2$ ✓.

questions.question: What is the trace (sum of diagonal entries) of $\begin{pmatrix} 5 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 4 & 0 \\ -1 & 6 & 7 \end{pmatrix}$?
questions.explanation: The trace is the sum of diagonal entries: $5 + 4 + 7 = 16$.

questions.question: A manufacturer uses the matrix $\begin{pmatrix} 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 4 \end{pmatrix}$ to represent units of product A and B produced at two factories. If production is tripled, what is the determinant of the new matrix?
questions.explanation: Original determinant: $2(4) - 3(1) = 5$. When scaled by factor 3, determinant is multiplied by $3^2 = 9$. New determinant: $5 \times 9 = 45$.
